title: 10 best herbal creams for baldness

remedy_properties: Inhibition of 5-alpha-reductase: Saw palmetto cream has been shown to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to hair loss, by blocking the activity of the enzyme 5-alpha-reductase.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Saw palmetto contains antioxidants like flavonoids and phytosterols, which help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation in the scalp, both of which can contribute to hair loss.
Enhancement of blood flow: Saw palmetto cream has been shown to increase blood flow to the scalp, which helps nourish the hair follicles and promote healthy hair growth.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Serenoa repens, also known as saw palmetto, creams helps with baldness because it blocks the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can contribute to hair loss. By reducing DHT levels, it slows down hair follicle shrinkage and promotes a healthy scalp environment. This herbal ingredient has been used for centuries to support hair growth and prevent baldness, making it a popular natural remedy for hair loss prevention. Its effectiveness has been backed by various studies, showcasing its potential in addressing male pattern baldness.
remedy_constituents: Fernandic acid: It is a triterpenoid saponin that inhibits 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme responsible for converting testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to hair loss.
Tormentic acid: It is a triterpenoid saponin that also inhibits 5-alpha-reductase, reducing DHT levels and slowing down hair loss.
Serenoa glycosides: These glycosides have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, promoting a healthy scalp environment that encourages hair growth and reduces hair loss.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the primary part used due to their high concentration of saw palmetto's active compounds, which help block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone linked to hair loss.
Leaves: Leaves are used as they contain a smaller amount of saw palmetto's active compounds, but are still effective in promoting hair growth and improving scalp health.
Stems: Stems are also utilized as they have some bioactive properties similar to the roots, although to a lesser extent.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried serenoa repens roots and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Grind the dried serenoa repens into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the powdered serenoa repens with 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 20-30 minutes or until it reaches a temperature of 180-190 degrees fahrenheit.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify for 30-40 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba contains flavonoids and terpenes that help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature aging and potentially lead to hair loss.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to have anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which is often a contributing factor to hair loss conditions like alopecia areata.

Improved blood circulation: The unique extract from Ginkgo biloba leaves (ginkgolide) can help improve blood flow and circulation, which is essential for delivering oxygen and nutrients to the hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with baldness because it increases blood flow to the scalp. This improved circulation helps promote hair growth by delivering essential nutrients and oxygen to the hair follicles. The antioxidants present in Ginkgo biloba creams also help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy scalp environment. Regular use of Ginkgo biloba creams may lead to thicker, fuller hair and a reduction in balding areas.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: Bilobalide is a sesquiterpene lactone that helps with baldness by promoting scalp health, reducing inflammation, and preventing hair loss caused by oxidative stress.

Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid that helps with baldness by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, promoting hair growth by increasing blood flow to the scalp, and protecting hair follicles from damage.

Ginkgo flavone glycosides: Ginkgo flavone glycosides are a group of flavonoid compounds that help with baldness by increasing blood flow to the scalp, reducing inflammation, and promoting hair growth by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are primarily used due to their high flavonoid and terpenoid content, which are believed to promote hair growth and improve blood circulation.
Buds: The buds are used for their flavonoid and terpene content, which may help to stimulate hair growth and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: The seeds are used due to their high content of bilobalide, a compound believed to promote hair growth and improve scalp health.
remedy_recipe: Extract 250 milliliters of ginkgo biloba extract from dried leaves using a solvent like glycerin or ethanol.
Mix 100 grams of emulsifying wax with 150 grams of shea butter in a double boiler at 65 degrees celsius.
Combine 10 milliliters of ginkgo biloba extract with 20 milliliters of distilled water and 5 grams of vitamin e oil.
Blend the emulsifying wax mixture with the ginkgo biloba extract mixture in a stainless steel bowl at 70 degrees celsius.
Pour the herbal ginkgo biloba cream into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and set for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Cicatrizone: This compound promotes collagen synthesis, which helps to strengthen hair follicles and improve hair growth by enhancing the structural integrity of the scalp.
Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation on the scalp that can contribute to hair loss and promote healthy hair growth.
Vitamin E: This essential nutrient has been shown to stimulate hair growth by improving blood circulation to the scalp, promoting collagen production, and protecting the hair follicles from damage.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with baldness because of its unique properties that promote hair growth and soothe the scalp. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, which nourish the hair follicles and stimulate hair growth. Additionally, aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce dandruff and itchiness, creating a healthy environment for hair to grow. Regular application of aloe vera creams can lead to thicker, healthier-looking hair.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates help stimulate hair growth by promoting the proliferation and differentiation of hair follicle cells.
Anthraquinones: These phenolic compounds, particularly aloe-emodin and aloin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, creating a conducive environment for hair growth.

Vitamins and Minerals (especially Vitamin E and Zinc): These nutrients play essential roles in hair growth, with Vitamin E acting as an antioxidant to protect hair follicles from damage and Zinc supporting the synthesis of keratin, a key protein in hair development.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their rich content of aloin, a compound that can help stimulate hair growth.
Stems: Used because they contain a high amount of gel, which is rich in vitamins and minerals that can promote healthy hair growth.
Roots: Used due to their high content of aloin and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of aloe vera gel from 2-3 mature aloe vera plants and store it in the refrigerator for later use.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 5 minutes stirring constantly.
Combine the aloe vera gel and the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture in a blender and blend until smooth for 2 minutes.
Add 10ml of distilled water and 5ml of vitamin e oil to the mixture and blend for another minute.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify completely at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Marine Silicon: Horsetail contains high levels of marine silicon, which helps to strengthen hair follicles and improve circulation, promoting thicker, fuller hair growth.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in horsetail cream help to combat oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to hair loss by protecting the scalp from damage and promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.

Mineral-Rich Formula: Horsetail is rich in minerals such as calcium, magnesium, and potassium, which nourish the scalp and promote healthy hair growth, helping to strengthen hair roots and prevent further balding.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense, also known as field horsetail, creams helps with baldness because it contains silicic acid, which promotes hair growth by strengthening hair follicles and improving scalp health. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce scalp irritation and inflammation, creating a favorable environment for hair growth. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help protect hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, leading to a thicker and healthier-looking hair. This natural approach can stimulate hair growth and reduce the risk of further hair loss.
remedy_constituents: Silicic acid: Silicic acid helps with baldness by promoting hair growth, strengthening hair follicles, and improving the overall health of hair.
Alkaloids (e.g., equisetin, equisetol): Alkaloids found in Equisetum arvense have been reported to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce scalp inflammation and promote hair growth.

Phenolic compounds (e.g., ferulic acid): Phenolic compounds in Equisetum arvense have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the scalp from oxidative stress, promote hair growth, and reduce the risk of hair loss.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used to make creams for baldness due to their high content of saponins, which may help stimulate hair growth.
Stems: Utilized in creams due to their potential ability to promote hair growth and prevent hair loss, possibly attributed to their saponin content.
Leaves: Used in creams because they may possess antioxidant properties that help promote a healthy scalp and stimulate hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-30 grams of fresh equisetum arvense, carefully cutting the stems above a node to encourage regrowth.
Dry the harvested equisetum arvense in a low-temperature oven at 50-60°c for 2-3 hours to preserve the herbal properties.
Infuse 10-15 grams of dried equisetum arvense in 100ml of cold-pressed carrier oil, such as coconut oil, for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, then filter the oil through a paper filter to remove any remaining particulate matter.
Mix 20-30ml of the herbal oil with 10-15 grams of beeswax and 20-30ml of distilled water in a double boiler, heating the mixture until the beeswax is fully incorporated.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage cream's antioxidants help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing oxidative stress that can contribute to hair loss.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in sage cream reduce swelling and irritation in the scalp, which can help alleviate conditions like alopecia areata and promote a healthy environment for hair growth.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Sage cream's antifungal and antibacterial agents help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause hair loss, such as ringworm and seborrheic dermatitis, allowing the scalp to heal and promoting new hair growth.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with baldness because of its rich antioxidant properties and ability to stimulate hair growth. The active compounds in sage, such as rosmarinic acid and camphor, have been shown to improve blood flow to the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair follicles. This, in turn, can help to strengthen existing hair, slow down hair loss, and even encourage new hair growth. The result is a thicker, fuller head of hair.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic Acid: This phenolic compound helps with baldness by promoting hair growth and reducing hair loss due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

Salvianolic Acid: This phenolic compound helps with baldness by inhibiting the activity of 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone into dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to hair loss.
Rosmarinic Acid: This phenolic compound helps with baldness by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to hair loss and promote hair growth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their rich content of antioxidants and essential oils that help promote hair growth.
Seeds: Utilized for their potential to stimulate hair growth and improve scalp health.
Flowers: Employed for their possible role in promoting blood circulation to the scalp, which may help stimulate hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh salvia officinalis leaves and flowers from your garden or a trusted supplier.
Steep 1/4 cup of the harvested leaves in 2 cups of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil for 2-4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Mix 1/4 cup of the infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Whip the cooled mixture with a hand mixer or electric beater until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Rosemary's antioxidant compounds help protect the scalp from free radical damage, which can contribute to hair loss and promote healthy hair growth.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can cause hair follicle damage and thinning, and instead promote a healthy environment for hair growth.
Stimulant Properties: Rosemary's stimulant properties increase blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth by delivering essential nutrients and oxygen to the hair follicles.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, creams helps with baldness because of its stimulating properties that promote hair growth. The active compounds in rosemary, such as carnosic acid, improve blood flow to the scalp, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy environment for hair follicles to grow. By increasing oxygenation and nutrient delivery, rosemary creams can help strengthen hair roots and slow down hair loss, ultimately leading to thicker, fuller hair and a reduction in baldness.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: A phenolic diterpene that helps prevent androgenetic alopecia by inhibiting 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme responsible for converting testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to hair loss.

Rosmarinic acid: A polyphenolic compound that has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the scalp, promoting hair growth and reducing hair loss.
Verbenone: A monoterpene with potential anti-androgenic and antioxidant effects, which may help block the production of DHT and reduce hair loss by promoting hair growth and improving scalp health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Rosmarinus officinalis contain essential oils that help stimulate hair growth and improve circulation to the scalp.
Flowers: The flowers of Rosmarinus officinalis are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that promote healthy hair growth and reduce dandruff.
Stems: The stems of Rosmarinus officinalis contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to strengthen hair follicles and reduce hair loss.
remedy_recipe: Harvest rosmarinus officinalis leaves from mature plants in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Combine 200g of rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 1l of carrier oil such as olive oil in a clean.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container and discard.
Mix 200g of beeswax with 100g of shea butter in a double boiler and heat the mixture until it.
Add 100g of the rosmarinus officinalis infused oil to the beeswax and shea butter mixture and.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidants help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing oxidative stress.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to hair loss conditions such as alopecia areata.
Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial agents help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can lead to hair loss, such as ringworm and folliculitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, creams helps with baldness because of its rich content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties. It promotes hair growth by stimulating blood flow to the scalp, improving the overall health of hair follicles. The thymol present in thyme cream enhances the growth phase of hair, while also reducing dandruff and itchiness associated with baldness. Regular application of thyme creams may lead to thicker and fuller hair growth, making it a potential natural remedy for baldness sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.
Thymol: A monoterpene that has antimicrobial properties, helping to control fungal infections such as ringworm (tinea capitis) and other scalp conditions that can lead to hair loss.
Carvacrol: A phenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect hair follicles from damage and promote hair growth by reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain thymol, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that can help promote a healthy scalp environment.
Stems: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help stimulate hair growth by improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation.
Roots: They contain sesquiterpenoids, which may have anti-androgenic effects that can help regulate hair growth and prevent baldness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried thymus vulgaris leaves in late morning when dew evaporates.
Combine 100g of dried thymus vulgaris leaves with 500ml of distilled water in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture for 45 minutes at 80°c to 90°c to release active compounds.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Mix 50g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter with 100ml of thymus vulgaris infusion in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Squalene: Squalene helps to moisturize and nourish the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth by reducing dryness and flakiness that can contribute to hair loss.
Oleic acid: Oleic acid is an anti-inflammatory agent that reduces inflammation in the scalp, which can cause hair loss and promote hair regrowth by improving blood flow and reducing oxidative stress.

Antioxidants (e.g., polyphenols): Antioxidants in olive creams help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature aging and hair loss, while also promoting overall scalp health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Olea europaea, also known as olive, creams helps with baldness because they stimulate hair growth, improve scalp health, and reduce inflammation. The antifungal and antibacterial properties of olive oil can help to prevent scalp conditions that may lead to hair loss, such as dandruff and itchiness. Additionally, the antioxidants in olive oil promote a healthy scalp environment, allowing hair follicles to thrive and produce thicker, fuller hair. Regular use of olive cream can lead to noticeable improvements in hair growth and density.
remedy_constituents: Oleuropein: This secoiridoid compound has been shown to promote hair growth by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, creating a favorable environment for hair follicle regeneration.

Tyrosol: As a phenolic compound, tyrosol has been found to stimulate hair growth by increasing the density of hair follicles and promoting the proliferation of dermal papilla cells, which are essential for hair growth.
Vitamin E (Tocopherol): This antioxidant helps to protect hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy scalp environment and supporting the growth of new hair.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They contain oleuropein, an active compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help promote hair growth.
Leaves: They contain oleuropein and other bioactive compounds that stimulate hair growth, reduce inflammation, and improve scalp health.
Barks: They contain oleuropein and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to promote hair growth and reduce hair loss.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh olea europaea leaves and stems at dawn or dusk when essential oil content is highest.
Steep the harvested olea europaea in 1l of boiling distilled water for 30 to 40 minutes to create a strong infusion.
Combine 100g of beeswax with 100g of shea butter and 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir 50g of the olea europaea infusion into the beeswax mixture and heat until the mixture reaches 60 to 70°c.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let the cream cool and thicken before pouring into containers.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcumin, reduce swelling and irritation on the scalp, which can contribute to hair loss by reducing blood flow and damaging hair follicles.
Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles and lead to baldness, promoting healthy hair growth and protecting against oxidative stress.

Stimulant properties: Turmeric's stimulatory compounds, such as gingerol and shilajit, promote blood flow and stimulate hair growth by increasing the production of keratinocytes, which are cells that produce keratin, a protein essential for hair growth.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with baldness because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound, curcumin, stimulates hair growth by increasing blood flow to the scalp and reducing oxidative stress. This leads to a healthier scalp environment, allowing dormant hair follicles to become active again. Curcuma longa creams also contain natural anti-androgen compounds, which can help block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone linked to hair loss.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: This polyphenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the scalp, promote hair growth, and prevent hair loss.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): A derivative of curcumin, DMC has been shown to have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help protect hair follicles from damage and promote hair growth.

Turmerone: A sesquiterpene present in Curcuma longa, turmerone has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce hair loss and promote hair growth by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the scalp.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the primary source of turmeric oil, which is used in creams to promote hair growth and reduce inflammation.
Roots: Roots contain curcumin, a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to stimulate hair growth and reduce baldness.
Seeds: Turmeric seeds contain a higher concentration of curcumin than roots or rhyzomes, making them a valuable ingredient in creams for baldness treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 mature curcuma longa rhizomes with a sharp knife after the harvest season has ended completely.
Dry the harvested rhizomes in a warm well-ventilated area for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of the powder with 50 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Stir in 5 grams of vitamin e oil and 2 grams of preservative until the mixture is smooth and creamy for 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Asiatic Pennywort's antioxidant compounds help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hair loss and promote healthy hair growth.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory agents reduce inflammation in the scalp, which is often a underlying cause of baldness and other hair-related issues, promoting a healthier environment for hair growth.

Stimulating capillary circulation: Asiatic Pennywort's vasodilatory effects help improve blood flow to the scalp, stimulating capillary circulation and nourishing the hair follicles with oxygen and nutrients, which can promote thicker, healthier hair.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica, also known as asiatic pennywort, creams helps with baldness because of its rich nutrient profile and potential to promote hair growth. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and saponins, are believed to stimulate blood flow to the scalp, nourish hair follicles, and prevent hair loss. By strengthening hair roots and promoting a healthy scalp environment, Centella asiatica creams may help to slow down or even reverse balding, making them a popular natural solution for those seeking to combat hair loss.
remedy_constituents: Asiatic acid: Helps with baldness by promoting hair growth and reducing inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to hair loss.
Madecassoside: Contributes to baldness treatment by enhancing blood flow to the scalp, reducing oxidative stress, and promoting the proliferation of hair follicle cells.

Madecassic acid: Assists in baldness treatment by inhibiting the production of 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to hair loss.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of triterpenoid saponins, which are believed to help stimulate hair growth.
Buds: Utilized for their potential to increase hair follicle density and promote hair growth.
Roots: Employed for their supposed ability to nourish and strengthen hair follicles, potentially leading to improved hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh centella asiatica leaves and flowers to use in the cream recipe.
Weigh and combine 50g of shea butter and 50g of cocoa butter in a double boiler.
Melt the shea and cocoa butter mixture over low heat for 10 minutes stirring occasionally.
Mix in 30g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil with the melted butter mixture.
Stir in 50g of distilled water and 10g of glycerin and let the mixture cool and thicken.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi creams helps with baldness because they stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth. The herb brahmi, also known as bacopa monnieri, has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to enhance cognitive function and promote hair growth. When applied topically, brahmi cream can nourish the hair follicles, strengthening and thickening individual strands, while also reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to baldness. Regular use of brahmi creams can lead to a fuller, thicker head of hair.
remedy_properties: Cognitive Enhancing Properties: Brahmi's cognitive-enhancing properties help stimulate hair growth by increasing blood flow to the scalp, which promotes healthy follicles and thickening of hair.
Antioxidant Properties: Brahmi's antioxidant properties help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.

Stress-Relieving Properties: Brahmi's stress-relieving properties help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can contribute to hair loss by slowing down the hair growth cycle; Brahmi creams can help regulate this cycle and promote thicker, fuller hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in promoting hair growth and reducing inflammation associated with baldness.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that stimulate hair follicle activity, leading to increased hair growth and density.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bacopa monnieri leaves and stems carefully for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a clean and well-ventilated area for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or blender.
Mix 250mg of ground bacopa monnieri with 50g of coconut oil and 25g of beeswax, heating until melted.
Pour the mixture into sterilized tubes or containers, allowing to cool and solidify within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with baldness because it stimulates blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties soothe an irritated scalp, reducing dandruff and itchiness that can contribute to hair loss. Additionally, stinging nettle's ability to balance hormone levels may help regulate excessive dihydrotestosterone (DHT) production, a common culprit behind male pattern baldness. By addressing these underlying issues, stinging nettle creams create an ideal environment for healthy hair growth, making them a natural and effective solution for promoting thicker, fuller locks.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Nettle cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, can reduce inflammation in the scalp, which is a common underlying cause of hair loss conditions like alopecia areata and telogen effluvium.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in nettle cream can help protect the scalp from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing the risk of hair thinning and loss.

Stimulating blood flow properties: Nettle's vasodilatory compounds, such as stachydrine and tremarina, can help increase blood flow to the scalp, which may promote healthy hair follicle function and stimulate hair growth by delivering essential nutrients and oxygen to the hair roots.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the stinging nettle plant have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in promoting hair growth and reducing inflammation associated with baldness.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of the stinging nettle plant contain bioactive compounds that stimulate hair follicle activity, leading to increased hair growth and improved scalp health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean dried nettle leaves by rinsing them gently with distilled water and pat drying with a paper towel for 10 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of dried nettle leaves with 40 milliliters of olive oil in a small saucepan and simmer over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding solids and let it cool to room temperature for 1 hour.
Mix the cooled nettle infusion with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of coconut oil in a small double boiler over low heat until melted and well combined for 15 minutes.
Remove from heat and stir until mixture thickens to a smooth, creamy consistency before pouring into sterilized containers and labeling for storage at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zucchini creams helps with baldness because of its unique blend of herbs and nutrients that promote hair growth and strengthen hair follicles. The rich antioxidants present in zucchini creams help to reduce oxidative stress on the scalp, leading to an increase in blood flow and oxygen supply to the hair roots. Additionally, the soothing properties of herbal extracts in the cream calm irritated scalps and promote a healthy environment for hair growth. This results in thicker, fuller hair that appears healthier and more vibrant.
remedy_properties: Biotin-rich herbs: Certain herbs like saw palmetto, ginseng, and fo-ti may help promote hair growth by increasing biotin levels in the body, which is essential for keratin production.
Anti-inflammatory herbs: Herbs like peppermint, rosemary, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an itchy scalp, reduce dandruff, and promote healthy hair growth.
Antioxidant-rich herbs: Antioxidant-rich herbs like ginkgo biloba, green tea, and turmeric may help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the zucchini plant have been traditionally used in herbal remedies to stimulate hair growth and prevent hair loss due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds.

Fruits: The fruits (zucchinis) contain a rich source of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that help promote healthy scalp conditions and reduce inflammation, making them effective in addressing baldness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean the cucurbita pepo leaves, ensuring they are free of contaminants and debris.
Combine 10 grams of dried cucurbita pepo leaves with 20 grams of coconut oil in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes to extract the active compounds from the leaves.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Allow the infused oil to cool to room temperature before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with baldness because of its unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory properties, and ability to stimulate blood flow. The antioxidants in lemongrass help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, while the anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and redness associated with balding conditions like alopecia areata. Additionally, lemongrass creams stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and strengthening existing follicles. This natural treatment can effectively address hair thinning and promote a fuller head of hair.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Lemongrass creams contain antioxidants that help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy hair growth.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemongrass creams reduce inflammation on the scalp, which can contribute to hair loss and balding, allowing for a healthier scalp environment.
Stimulating Blood Flow: Lemongrass essential oils have been shown to stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy circulation and delivering oxygen and nutrients to the hair follicles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lemongrass roots help reduce oxidative stress and promote healthy scalp conditions.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground storage organs of the lemongrass plant contain high amounts of citral, a compound that stimulates hair growth and improves circulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Combine the harvested leaves with 200 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler, heating it at 60 degrees celsius for three hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solids, discarding any remaining plant material.
Add 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter to the strained oil mixture, stirring until fully incorporated.
Pour the final mixture into sterilized glass jars and let it cool at room temperature for 24 hours before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas Coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like ursolic acid, which can help reduce inflammation in the scalp and promote healthy hair growth by reducing redness and irritation.

Antioxidant properties: The cream's antioxidant properties, thanks to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthier environment for hair growth.

Stimulating hormone production: Kansas Coneflower has been shown to stimulate the production of hormones that promote hair growth, such as testosterone and dihydrotestosterone (DHT), which can help counteract the effects of male/female pattern baldness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing scalp irritations and promoting healthy hair growth.
Seeds: Containing fatty acids and other nutrients, seeds help to nourish and strengthen hair follicles, promoting thicker and fuller hair.
remedy_recipe: Gather organic echinacea angustifolia roots dried in shade or air-dried for at least six months.
Combine one part dried echinacea angustifolia root with two parts olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture by storing it in a cool dark place for 14 days stirring daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another container and discard solids after three hours.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax melted at 180°f for 15 minutes to create a creamy consistency taking about an hour.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with baldness because it is enriched with the medicinal properties of Echinacea, a natural herb that has been used for centuries to promote hair growth. The cream's active ingredients stimulate blood flow to the scalp, nourishing and revitalizing follicles, while also strengthening hair roots and promoting new hair growth. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help to soothe and protect the scalp, creating a healthy environment for hair to thrive.

Licorice creams helps with baldness because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to promote hair growth by stimulating blood flow to the scalp. Additionally, licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy hair growth. Regular use of herbal licorice creams may also help to strengthen hair follicles, leading to thicker, fuller-looking hair.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the scalp, which can contribute to hair loss by promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.

Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals and promote a healthy blood flow to the scalp.
Saponins: These natural detergents help to stimulate hair growth by increasing the production of collagen, improving blood circulation, and enhancing the strength and elasticity of hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that promotes hair growth and reduces inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Contain triterpenoids that inhibit 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme involved in male pattern baldness.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and combine 100g of glycerin with 200g of distilled water in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Boil 50g of dried licorice root (glycyrrhiza glabra) in 500ml of distilled water for 15 minutes then strain the mixture.
Mix the cooled licorice infusion with 100g of beeswax and 20g of sweet almond oil in a double boiler until melted.
Add 10% by weight of liquid paraffin and stir well to combine with the mixture.
Pour the cream base into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and thicken for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng creams helps with baldness because it stimulates blood flow to the scalp, promoting hair growth by nourishing the follicles. The cream's active ingredients, including ginsenosides and panaxosides, help to block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to hair loss. Additionally, ginseng creams may help to increase the length of the anagen phase, the active growth stage of hair, leading to thicker, fuller-looking locks. As a result, ginseng creams can provide a natural and effective solution for those experiencing baldness or thinning hair.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginseng's antioxidants help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hair loss and thinning.
Immunomodulatory effects: Ginseng has been shown to modulate the immune response, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.
Vasodilatory properties: Ginseng's ability to dilate blood vessels increases oxygenation and nutrient delivery to the scalp, promoting healthy hair follicles and preventing balding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that stimulate hair growth and promote a healthy scalp.
Rhizomes: Rhizomes contain high concentrations of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Measure and mix 20 grams of pure beeswax with 10 grams of coconut oil in a heat-resistant container.
Add 15 grams of finely ground dried panax ginseng root powder to the mixture and stir well to combine ingredients.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and combined.
Remove the container from the heat and let it cool and thicken for 10-15 minutes before whipping with an electric mixer for 2 minutes.
Transfer the herbal panax ginseng cream to a clean glass jar and store in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with baldness because of its unique ability to stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting a healthy hair growth environment. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and flakiness associated with conditions like psoriasis and eczema that can contribute to hair loss. Additionally, the antioxidants present in pot marigold creams help to strengthen hair follicles and nourish the scalp, leading to thicker, healthier-looking locks.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, can reduce inflammation in the scalp, which may contribute to hair loss by blocking the growth of new hair follicles.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold cream, including beta-carotene and lutein, can help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing the risk of baldness.

Wound-healing properties: Pot marigold's wound-healing compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can aid in the repair and regeneration of damaged scalp tissue, which may help promote hair regrowth and reduce balding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent in anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with hair loss conditions.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants, which help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried calendula flowers and gently dry them using air circulation for 1-2 hours.
Grind the dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 5 minutes.
Combine 100 grams of the powdered calendula with 50 grams of sweet almond oil, 20 grams of coconut oil, and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30-40 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches a temperature of 120°f to 140°f.
Remove the cream from heat and allow it to cool and thicken at room temperature for about 2 hours before transferring it to an airtight container.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for baldness are natural topical treatments that help stimulate hair growth, improve scalp health, and reduce inflammation, ultimately addressing the causes of baldness. The best herbal creams for baldness include Aloe barbadensis, Ginkgo biloba, Serenoa repens, Panax ginseng, Urtica dioica, Curcuma longa, Rosmarinus officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Cassia auriculata, and Aloe vera. If left untreated, baldness can significantly impact one's life, leading to low self-esteem, social withdrawal, and even depression.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to baldness that are treated with herbal creams are acne, eczema, and psoriasis. These conditions cause skin inflammation, redness, and scaling, which can lead to hair loss or thinning. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and chamomile can help soothe the skin, reduce inflammation, and promote healthy hair growth. Additionally, herbal creams may be used to treat scalp conditions such as dandruff, seborrheic dermatitis, and lichen planus.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with baldness is a blend of Saw Palmetto, Biotin, and Rosemary extracts. Saw Palmetto helps block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to hair loss. Biotin promotes hair growth by strengthening hair follicles, while Rosemary extract improves circulation and stimulates hair regrowth. When applied topically, these herbal creams can help stimulate hair growth, reduce inflammation, and promote a healthy scalp environment, ultimately leading to thicker, fuller hair. Regular use can lead to noticeable results.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, the review aimed to collect results of experimental studies related to alopecia, to screen previously documented combinations of herbs claimed to be effective, and to uncover other useful information that will guide the clinical application and scientific research of drug combinations or individual herbs for the treatment of alopecia. The study reviewed experimental studies on the methods used and the mechanisms of action of the herbs and constituent compounds, searching for combinations of herbs and their constituent compounds used in identified studies. The study found that among 108 herbal combinations screened, Angelica, Rehmannia glutinosa, Ligusticum chuanxiong hort, and Radix Rehmanniae had a high number of occurrences, and their association with vascular drugs was also found to be close, suggesting that herbal creams may be a good option for treating baldness due to their high efficacy and safety profile.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with baldness because of its unique properties that promote hair growth and reduce shedding. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, which nourish the scalp and stimulate hair follicles. Regular application of Aloe vera cream can increase blood flow to the scalp, reduce dandruff and itchiness, and promote a healthy environment for hair growth, ultimately leading to thicker and fuller hair.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: Helps with baldness by promoting hair growth through its anti-inflammatory properties, reducing scalp irritation and promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Anthraquinones: Contributes to baldness treatment by inhibiting 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone associated with hair loss.
Saponins: Assists in baldness treatment by enhancing blood flow to the scalp, promoting hair growth, and reducing hair loss due to improved scalp health and reduced oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves contain aloin, a compound that can help stimulate hair growth and reduce inflammation.
Gel from Leaves: The gel inside aloe vera leaves has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an itchy scalp and promote healthy hair growth.
Extract from Leaves: Aloe vera leaf extract contains vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that can help nourish and strengthen hair follicles, promoting hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Extract 200g of fresh aloe barbadensis gel from 2-3 mature leaves and strain it through cheesecloth.
Mix the extracted gel with 20g of vegetable glycerin and 10g of distilled water in a bowl.
Add 10g of beeswax and 5g of emulsifying wax to the mixture and heat it to 70c for 10 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken to a consistency suitable for cream formation for 20-30 minutes.
Whip the cooled mixture with a hand mixer until it reaches a smooth and creamy texture in 5 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with baldness because of its potential to improve blood circulation to the scalp, promoting a healthy hair growth environment. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in Ginkgo biloba may also help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to hair loss. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba creams may help to stimulate hair follicles, encouraging new hair growth and potentially slowing down the progression of baldness. This natural approach may offer a promising solution for individuals experiencing hair loss.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These antioxidants help to improve blood circulation and reduce oxidative stress, promoting a healthy scalp environment conducive to hair growth.
Bilobalide: This triterpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce scalp inflammation and promote a healthy hair growth environment.
Ginkgolides: These diterpene lactones have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce scalp inflammation and promote hair growth by increasing blood flow to the scalp.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to promote hair growth and improve scalp health.
Seeds: They contain bilobalide and ginkgolides, which are thought to increase blood flow to the scalp and reduce inflammation.
Barks: They are used to create extracts rich in flavonoids, which may help to strengthen hair follicles and promote hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh ginkgo biloba leaves and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Infuse the ginkgo biloba leaves in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut oil at 200f for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter to the infused oil and heat it gently.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify completely for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Serenoa repens creams helps with baldness because they contain saw palmetto, a natural herb that blocks the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to hair loss. By inhibiting DHT, Serenoa repens creams promote a healthy scalp environment, preventing hair follicles from shrinking and promoting hair growth. Additionally, saw palmetto has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the scalp, creating a favorable condition for hair regrowth, making it an effective solution for individuals experiencing baldness.
remedy_constituents: Fatty Acids: Help block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to hair loss and baldness.
Phenolic Acids: Inhibit the activity of 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme responsible for converting testosterone to DHT, thereby slowing down hair loss.
Terpenoids: Exhibit anti-androgenic properties, reducing the levels of DHT and preventing it from binding to androgen receptors in hair follicles, promoting hair growth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of saponins, which are believed to promote hair growth and improve scalp health.
Roots: The roots of Serenoa repens contain saponins and other compounds that may help stimulate hair growth and prevent hair loss.
Fruits: Although less commonly used, some products may utilize the fruits or berries of Serenoa repens due to their potential ability to promote hair growth and improve scalp health.
remedy_recipe: Combine 10 grams of dried serenoa repens in 500 milliliters of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Place the jar in a warm location at 40 degrees celsius for 2-3 weeks with occasional stirring.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean container.
Add 20 grams of beeswax to 100 milliliters of the infused oil and heat the mixture until melted.
Pour the melted mixture into small tin containers and allow it to cool completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng creams helps with baldness because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that promote a healthy scalp environment. The ginsenosides present in Panax ginseng stimulate hair growth by improving blood circulation and reducing dandruff. Additionally, it has been shown to prevent hair loss caused by stress, fatigue, and hormonal imbalances. By nourishing the scalp and promoting a balanced hair growth cycle, Panax ginseng creams can help to thicken and restore hair, making it an effective natural remedy for baldness.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These constituents have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce hair loss and promote hair growth by protecting the scalp from damage.
Terpenoids: Terpenoids in Panax ginseng have been shown to stimulate hair growth, reduce hair loss, and promote the regeneration of hair follicles, leading to improved hair density.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the scalp from damage, reduce hair loss, and promote the growth of new hair follicles.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are primarily used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, particularly ginsenosides, which are believed to stimulate hair growth and improve scalp health.
Leaves: They contain similar bioactive compounds to the roots and are often used as a cost-effective alternative in hair growth creams.
Buds: They are used due to their higher concentration of ginsenosides compared to the leaves and are believed to have a more potent effect on hair growth and scalp health.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 30 grams of dried panax ginseng root and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 5 minutes.
Combine 15 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until melted for 10 minutes.
Add 30 grams of distilled water to the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture and stir until combined.
Add the ground panax ginseng powder to the mixture and stir continuously for 5 minutes over low heat.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and thicken for 30 minutes before pouring into jars.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica creams helps with baldness because they contain stinging nettle's unique combination of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties soothe the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth by reducing redness and irritation. Urtica dioica's rich nutrient profile, including silica, zinc, and iron, helps strengthen hair follicles and improve overall scalp health. As a result, the cream can help slow down hair loss and even stimulate new hair growth, making it a popular natural remedy for baldness and thinning hair.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: Help with baldness by stimulating hair growth, improving scalp circulation, and reducing inflammation associated with hair loss.
Alkaloids (specifically, berberine): Help with baldness by reducing oxidative stress, inhibiting 5-alpha-reductase enzyme activity, and promoting hair growth.
Phenolic acids (specifically, caffeic acid and ferulic acid): Help with baldness by reducing inflammation, improving blood flow to the scalp, and protecting hair follicles from damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which help promote hair growth and improve scalp health.
Roots: They are believed to have hormone-balancing properties, which may help stimulate hair growth and slow down hair loss.
Stems: They contain a compound called urticine, which has been shown to promote hair growth and strengthen hair follicles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh urtica dioica leaves and flowers when they are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested leaves and flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1/2 cup of dried urtica dioica with 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 15 minutes stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and pour it into a glass jar to cool and solidify.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with baldness because they contain turmeric's active compound, curcumin, which stimulates blood flow to the scalp. This increased circulation helps to promote hair growth by delivering essential nutrients and oxygen to the hair follicles. Curcumin's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce oxidative stress and prevent damage to the hair follicles, creating an environment conducive to healthy hair growth. Regular application of Curcuma longa creams can lead to thicker, fuller hair and potentially slow down or even reverse baldness.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin helps with baldness by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to hair loss and promote hair growth.

Tumerone (also known as Turmerone or Alpha-Turmerone): Tumerone helps with baldness by promoting hair growth by stimulating the proliferation of hair follicle cells and increasing blood flow to the scalp.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): Demethoxycurcumin helps with baldness by reducing the formation of dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can contribute to hair loss and baldness.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Used due to their high content of curcumin, a bioactive compound that may help in promoting hair growth and reducing inflammation.
Roots: Utilized for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in promoting hair growth and strengthening hair follicles.
Seeds: Employed for their potential in stimulating hair growth due to the presence of certain compounds that may help in promoting cell division and hair development.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30g of fresh curcuma longa rhizomes and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Grind the rhizomes into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 2g of the ground curcuma longa powder with 10g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 160-180°f for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax is fully incorporated.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar for 5 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams helps with baldness because they contain rosmarinic acid, a potent antioxidant that promotes hair growth by stimulating blood circulation to the scalp. This increased blood flow brings essential nutrients to the hair follicles, encouraging healthy hair growth and preventing hair loss. Additionally, rosmarinic acid has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce stress and promote a healthy scalp environment, ideal for hair growth and maintenance. This makes Rosmarinus officinalis creams a popular natural remedy for baldness and hair loss.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: This constituent helps promote hair growth by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to hair loss.
Carnosol: This constituent has been shown to inhibit 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can contribute to hair loss.
Rosmarinic acid: This constituent has antioxidant properties that help protect hair follicles from damage and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are used due to their high concentration of essential oils and antioxidants that promote hair growth and improve scalp health.
Stems: The stems are used to create a base for the creams, allowing the active ingredients from the leaves to be more easily absorbed by the scalp.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their fragrance and potential hair growth-promoting properties, although the scientific evidence for this is limited.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1/4 cup of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl and discard the solids.
Mix 1/2 cup of distilled water with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and heat in a double boiler.
Combine the infused liquid with 1/4 cup of shea butter and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and reaches a creamy consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with baldness because of its unique ability to stimulate hair growth and reduce stress-related hair loss. The antioxidants and essential oils present in the cream promote a healthy scalp, reducing inflammation and promoting a favorable environment for hair growth. The calming properties of the lavender also help to reduce stress, a common contributor to hair loss. As a result, the cream can help to improve hair density and reduce the appearance of baldness.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing hair loss caused by inflammation in the scalp.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits antioxidant properties, protecting hair follicles from oxidative stress and promoting hair growth.
Lavandulol: Demonstrates anti-androgenic effects, helping to reduce hair loss caused by hormonal imbalances.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to promote hair growth and soothe scalp irritations.
Flowers: They are used because of their antiseptic and anti-fungal properties, which help to prevent scalp infections and promote a healthy scalp environment for hair growth.
Stems: They are used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, similar to the leaves, which help to soothe scalp irritations and promote hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of dried lavender flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil like sweet almond oil at 70-80 degrees celsius for 6 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to separate the solids from the liquid.
Melt 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof glass bowl.
Combine the melted beeswax mixture with the infused lavender oil and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil in a large mixing bowl.
Whip the mixture with a handheld electric mixer until it thickens and becomes a smooth, creamy texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia auriculata creams helps with baldness because it contains flavonoids and saponins, which stimulate hair growth and improve scalp health. The antifungal and antibacterial properties of the cream help to reduce inflammation and promote a healthy environment for hair growth. Additionally, the cream's antioxidant properties help to protect the hair follicles from damage, leading to thicker and fuller hair. Regular use of Cassia auriculata cream can help to slow down or even reverse hair loss, resulting in a fuller and more youthful-looking head of hair.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help with baldness by promoting hair growth due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Phenolic acids: These compounds help with baldness by inhibiting dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to hair loss and balding.
Triterpenoids: These plant compounds help with baldness by promoting hair growth and reducing inflammation in the scalp.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their potential to stimulate hair growth and promote scalp health.
Barks: They are utilized for their possible anti-inflammatory properties that may aid in reducing hair loss and promoting new hair growth.
Roots: They are employed for their possible tonic and stimulating effects on the scalp, which may help to promote hair growth and reduce baldness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cassia auriculata flowers and leaves in large quantities for later use in processing.
Dry the harvested cassia auriculata flowers and leaves in a warm air oven for 2 hours at 50 degrees celsius.
Grind the dried cassia auriculata flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the cassia auriculata powder with coconut oil in a 1:3 ratio by weight and heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes at 60 degrees celsius.
Strain the cooled mixture through a cheesecloth and store it in airtight containers for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with baldness because of its rich antioxidant properties and ability to stimulate blood circulation to the scalp. The soothing and calming effects of aloe vera promote a healthy scalp environment, reducing inflammation and promoting hair growth. Aloe vera's vitamin E and folic acid content also help to nourish and strengthen hair follicles, reducing the risk of hair loss and promoting a thicker, fuller head of hair. Regular use of aloe vera creams can lead to noticeable improvements in hair growth.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These constituents help with baldness by stimulating blood flow to the scalp, promoting hair growth, and reducing inflammation that can contribute to hair loss.
Polysaccharides: These constituents help with baldness by promoting hair growth by stimulating the production of collagen, a protein essential for hair health, and reducing oxidative stress.
Flavonoids: These constituents help with baldness by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to hair loss, and promoting the growth of new hair follicles.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Aloe vera are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of aloin and aloe-emodin, which are believed to promote hair growth and prevent hair loss.
Stems: The stems of Aloe vera are also used due to their richness in vitamins and minerals, particularly vitamin E and folic acid, which help to nourish the scalp and promote hair growth.
Seeds: Aloe vera seeds are used in some creams due to their antioxidant properties, which help to protect the scalp from damage and promote a healthy environment for hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Melt 1/2 cup of shea butter and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler at 120-140 degrees fahrenheit.
Combine 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel and 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil with the melted shea butter mixture.
Add 2 teaspoons of beeswax to the mixture and stir until it dissolves completely in 2 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to 100-110 degrees fahrenheit in 30 minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into a container and let it solidify at room temperature for 2 hours.
